methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Windborne is a Series A climate and energy company that has raised $23.4M to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector includes 9 peer companies—1 public (Lonza, ticker LNZA) and 8 private firms including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with aligned valuation tiers to support a reliable median-multiple approach; a sector-wide median would introduce substantial noise.

The applicable method is stage-progression valuation. Using the $23.4M total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to later funding rounds yields an implied valuation of $47M. This reflects the expected value creation pattern between early institutional rounds and subsequent growth stages.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $40M (low) to $54M (high), with a median of $47M. The analysis rests on a single methodological pillar, making it sensitive to the stage-progression multiplier assumption. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for Windborne's specific risk profile and market dynamics within frontier climate tech, as well as the absence of granular peer valuation data at comparable stages. Additional diligence on comparable Series B or later-stage outcomes in the sector would strengthen conviction.
